content.problemStatement:
One of Chef's friends offered him a deal: during DDD days, they are going to exchange money. For each iii (1≤i≤D1 \le i \le D1≤i≤D), on the iii-th day, Chef's friend would give Chef AAA rupees, while Chef would give his friend 2i−12^{i-1}2i−1 rupees (111 rupee on day 111, 222 rupees on day 222, 444 rupees on day 333, and so on). Chef's profit from the deal is the total number of rupees he received from his friend minus the total number of rupees he gave his friend.

Chef decided to ask for your advice before accepting the deal. You want to help him by telling him two numbers D1D_1D1​ and D2D_2D2​, where D1D_1D1​ is the maximum value of DDD such that Chef should accept the deal, i.e. his profit from the deal is positive if D=D1D = D_1D=D1​, and D2D_2D2​ is the value of DDD that leads to the maximum possible profit for Chef. If there are multiple values of DDD that lead to the maximum profit, D2D_2D2​ is the smallest of these values.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer AAA.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers D1D_1D1​ and D2D_2D2​.
